def: s.m. Any unlucky omen or portent, a bad omen.

adj. (f. -ā), Hidden, secret; not clear; unintelligible.
s.m. The falling sickness, epilepsy.
adj. (f. -ā), Helpless, destitute, without shelter

def: adj. Disgraceful, disreputable; wicked
s.f. Accident, misfortune, calamity, disaster, evil, danger, trouble, adversity. — āpat-kāl, s.m. A season of trouble or distress.

s.m. Absence of a road; a wrong path; error.
adj. (f. -ā), Unwholesome; indigestible.
s.f. Disrepute, dishonour, disgrace.
s.f. An unchaste wife or woman, a courtezan.
adj. Faithless, treacherors.
adj. (f. -ī), Faithless, treacherous.
s.m. (f. -ā), An inferior, undeserving or worthless person
s.f. آپت āpat, q.v.
adj. Awkward, uncouth; unskilled, incapable; ineloquent; sick, diseased.
s.m. Offspring; descendant; race; a patronymical affix. — apatya-dharmma, s.m. Duty to offspring; the duty of offspring to their progenitors. — apatya-vāćak-sangyā, s.f. (in Gram.) A patronymic noun.
adj. Relating to offspring; (in Gram.) relating to the formation of patronymic nouns.
adj. (f. -ā), Unmanly, cowardly, effeminate; impotent; — superhuman.
s.m. Unmanliness; inhumanity, &c.
s.m. Censure, blame, reproach; complaint, accusation; calumny.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Censuring, blaming, reproaching; complaining against, accusing; slandering; — complainer, accuser, plaintiff.
adj. (f. -ā), Defiling, polluting; impure.
s.m. Completion; liberation, deliverance; emancipation of the soul from the body and exemption from further transmigration; final beatitude.
adj. (f. -ā), Independent, absolute, uncontrolled.
adj. (f. -ā), Independent, absolute, uncontrolled.
adj. (f. -ā), Unclean, unholy; impure, defiled.
s.f. Uncleanness, impurity; unholiness.
s.m. Destruction; death; injury; loss
s.m. Disrepute, disgrace, infamy.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Disreputable, disgraceful, infamous; unfortunate; — a disreputable person, &c.
s.f. = اپسرا apsarā, q.v. s.f. A female dancer in the court of Indra; a nymph; a courtesan. — apsarā-swar, s.m. The notes or echoes of the apsarās.

r: ape, āp, ape-soṅ ape, āp-se āp
def: (prov.) = آپ āp, q.v. — ape-soṅ ape = āp-se āp, q.v. s.v. āp. s.m. Water (cf. P. آب āb).
adj. (f. -ā), Invisible, unseen.

s.f. Consideration, regard, reference, relation; comparison; waiting, expectation, hope; — postpos. (govern. gen. -kī), In reference (to), in respect (of), in comparison (with). — apekshā rakhnā (-kī), To hope (for); entertain the desire (of); to look (to), have need (of); to need, require. — apekshā karnā (-kī), To be in expectation or anticipation (of), to wait (for), to expect; to hope (for).
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Waiting for, expecting; — an expectant.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī) = اپيکشك apekshak, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Waiting for, expecting; — an expectant.
part. adj. Expected, waited (for), hoped (for); required.
adj. Not to be pushed out of the way; immovable.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Unfit to drink; — forbidden liquor.
adj. (f. -ā), Unfruitful, barren, unproductive, fruitless, unprofitable, ineffectual, useless.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Not in fruit.
s.f. Barrenness, unproductiveness.
s.m. A subdivision of the caste or tribe called ahīr, q.v.

v.n. To swell out, be distended (the stomach); to be satiated, gorged, overloaded (the stomach); to become corpulent; to exceed bounds; to be puffed up with pride; to be unduly elated with success; to boast, swagger; to be or become very rich (cf. aphrā jānā)
subs: v.n. To move or walk with a swaggering gait, to strut.

def: adj. Not frothy, without foam.
adj. (f. -ī), Very puffed up; self-conceited, opinionated; obstinate.
s.f. Opium.

part. adj. (f. -ī), Swollen, puffed out; filled to overloading (the stomach); puffed up with pride, elated, &c. — aphrā jānā, v.n. To be or become glutted, cloyed, gorged, &c.; to be puffed up with pride; to be elated.
s.f. Surfeit, over-feeding, gorging; gluttony; distention of the stomach (from over-feeding); superfluity; superabundance, &c. (see aphrānā).
v.t. To feed (one) till his stomach swells, to cram, glut, satiate; — to give one (money, &c.) to his heart's desire
s.f. Opium.
adv. Moreover, also, besides, even, still, though, yet; assuredly.
s.f. Appeal. — apīl karnā, To appeal.
adj. (f. -ā), Not fat, lean; light.
s.m. Dryness of the nose; loss of smell; a cold.
adj. (f. -ā), Without pain.
adj. (f. -ā), Without pain.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Unfit to be drunk; forbidden to be taken as drink (see next).
v.s.v. آپ āp.
s.m. Pouring upon; bathing, ablution.
s.m. Bathing, ablution; diving, submerging ( = āplāva, q.v.). s.m. Pouring upon; bathing, ablution.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Bathed, sprinkled, washed: — s.m. An initiated householder.
adj. (f. -ī), Of or belonging to self, own (one's, his, &c.
subs: relatives, friends, &c — apnā apnā, adj. (f. -ī), Each his own; respective; particular, special; individual
To be keen each after his own interests or affairs
Each in its proper place; respective or assigned places
(or bigāna), s.m. Relations and strangers; friends and foes
adv. With a face peculiarly blank, or foolish-looking, or expressive of shame (e.g. apnā-sā muṅh le-kar āyā, 'he came with a peculiarly blank expression, or an expression of shame on his face')
To do all in one's power
On one's own part or side

or apnā kar-lenā, v.t. To make (a person or thing) one's own; to appropriate to one's use, possess oneself of, help oneself to; to usurp; to embezzle; to gain an ascendency over (a person), win (a person) over to one's side, attach (a person) to oneself, make (another) one's creature
adv. As, or as carefully as, one's own
To reap the fruit of one's own misdeeds, to reap what one has sown
To worship self, think much of oneself, to be vain or proud
To glory or revel in one's own means, to rejoice in one's independence; to be indifferent to fortune, to be independent
apnā kiyā pānā = apnī karnī bharnī
q.v. — apnī gānā, To sing one's own praises, blow one's own trumpet, to egotize, boast, brag; to be full of one's own affairs or grievances, &c.
To sleep as or when one pleases; to be one's own master; to be free from all care or anxiety
To dig one's own grave, be one's own destroyer or enemy
To chop off one's own hand, be one's own destroyer or enemy
To become one's own; to become attached to one; to become one's creature; to behave like a relation, act as one's own kindred.
ex: دوسروں کو دیکھنے سے پہلے اپنا خیال رکھو۔
dusrõ ko dekhne se pahle apnā xayāl rakho.
Before checking on the others make sure that you yourself are fine.
wety: Cognate with Southern Hindko اپڑا (apṛā) and Punjabi اپݨا (apṇā).

On one's own part or side

or apnā kar-lenā, v.t. To make (a person or thing) one's own; to appropriate to one's use, possess oneself of, help oneself to; to usurp; to embezzle; to gain an ascendency over (a person), win (a person) over to one's side, attach (a person) to oneself, make (another) one's creature
adv. As, or as carefully as, one's own

To sleep as or when one pleases; to be one's own master; to be free from all care or anxiety

To chop off one's own hand, be one's own destroyer or enemy

def: v.t. To make (a thing, &c.) one's own, to convert to one's use, &c. ( = apnā karnā, q.v. s.v. apnā).

s.f. Relationship, kinship; intimacy, friendship
= H آپنو आपनौ āpnau, āpnauṅ, (Braj) = اپنا apnā, q.v.
s.f. Absence of splendour; darkness; dulness.

adj. (f. -ā), Without dignity; — not strong, not violent, not formidable.
adj. (f. -ā), Modern, recent.
adj. (f. -ā), Not chief, subordinate; powerless; little; mean.

adj. (f. -ā), Unmanifested; not apparent.
adj. (t. -ā), Without proof; untrue.
adj. (f. -ā), Unauthentic, without authority.

adj. (f. -inī), Inanimate, lifeless.
part. Unobtained.

part. adj. Unattainable.

s.m. Disapprobation, disfavour.

adj. (f. -inī), Dull; blunt; powerless; unfortunate; undignified; mean.

adj. (f. -ā), Imperceptible, invisible, not evident; not present, absent; secret.

s.f. Want of experience; unskilfulness; stupidity.
s.m. Want of love, dislike, enmity, hatred.
s.f. Unfriendliness, dislike, enmity, hatred.

s.f. A quotient; an equation of a degree.
adj. (f. -ā), Tail-less.
s.f. The tree Dalbergia sisn.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Irreverent, irreligious; not deserving reverence (a god), not deserving respect (a man); — an irreligious person, &c.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Not to be worshipped or revered; unlawful or unsuitable as an object of worship.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Unclean, unholy; irreligious; void of merit; wicked; — unholiness; irreligiousness, wickedness.
= اپورو apūrva, q.v. adj. (f. -ā), Unprecedented, unparalleled, unheard of, uncommon, remarkable, extraordinary, wonderful; eminent; new, strange.

def: s.f. A goad, a sharp probe at the end of a whip or stick, pricker, bodkin; spur of a cock; a kind of ladle used in sugar-factories
subs: (-ke), — mārnā (-ko), To prick with a goad, to goad; to spur.

def: s.f. Obstruction, interruption, stoppage, constipation; bar, barrier, barricade; embankment, dike; dam or weir thrown across a stream to catch fish; — opposition, contrariety; obstinacy; contention, dispute; (in comp.) adj. & adv. Cross-, counter-, out of the way, remote, &c.
subs: (in W. and S. India), s.m. A distant relative; a pretended relative
adj. Uneven, rough, rugged; unmeaning, nonsensical (in this and the following senses the word is probably connected with baṛbaṛ, and not with aṛ); s.m. Senseless words or talk, nonsense, raving
v.n. To talk inconsiderately or incoherently, to talk nonsense; to rave
s.m. Cross-pole, bar, barrier, barricade, boom; stoppage, hindrance, impediment
s.f. Counter-security; surety for a surety; mutual responsibility
v.n. To be obstinate, head-strong, restive; to be obstructive; to o pose, to stick, to gib (as a horse)
(W. and S. India), s.m. A Hindū who follows a trade different from that of his ancestors; — aṛ-māp, (W. and S. India) s.f. A measure in excess of the standard

ety: S. आ+वरण or वृति, rt. वृ
def: s.f. Covering, concealment, screen, shelter, protection, guard, shield, defence, refuge, security; prop, support, buttress; obstruction, interruption, stoppage, obstacle, hindrance, impediment, bar, barrier; fence, wall; check, restraint, prohibition; mound or bank of earth forming the boundary of a field; a horizontal or curved line painted across the forehead
subs: (or aṛ)band or bondh, s.m. A cloth worn by Hindūs, passing round the waist and between the thighs, and fastened behind
(-kī), adv. Under cover (of); lying in ambush; behind; across, athwart, obliquely, transversely; under the pretext or pretence (of)
(-kī), To come in the way (of); to stop the way; to come between, intervene, interpose.

def: A kind of ladle used in sugar manufactories ( = آر ār, q.v.) s.m. Spoke of a wheel ( = ārā, q.v.). s.m. Spoke of a wheel; a saw; a shoe-maker's knife or awl
subs: To be tortured, tormented, put to excruciating pain, &c.

def: s.f. A bird, the Sarali, Turdus ginginianus.

ety: v.n. fr. عير 'to blame, or reproach,' &c.
def: s.f. Disgrace, reproach, ignominy, shame; bashfulness, modesty
subs: or ʻār lagnā (ko), To have a feeling of, shame, to feel ashamed.

ety: contrac. of اگر agar, — used in poetry
def: conj. If.

ety: fr. āwardan, 'to bring,' — used in comp.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Bringing; bringer (e.g. bar-ār, 'fruit-bringing,' 'fertile').
s.m. Spoke of a wheel; a saw; a shoe-maker's knife or awl
subs: To be tortured, tormented, put to excruciating pain, &c.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Interrupting, interposing; opposing; obstructive; cross, transverse, oblique, crooked

s.f. Opposition, contrariety, perverseness, obstinacy; peevishness, crossness
āṛī karnā, To obstruct, check; to oppose, cross, thwart
s.f. Transverse wall; a wall made to stop a way
s.f. A chain thrown across to obstruct
(or aṛe)ānā, v.t. & v.n. To come between, intervene, interpose; to shield, protect, shelter, screen; — to come under shelter, to be protected

(or hāthoṅ) lenā or le-lenā (-ko), To fold the arms round, to embrace, hug; to clutch, gripe; to pull up; to vanquish, overcome (as in wrestling); to fall foul of; to rebuke, chide; to revile; to shame, disgrace, humiliate
To be or become crooked or bent; to bend or double oneself.

def: s.m. A saw, &c. = آرا ārā, q.v. s.m. Spoke of a wheel; a saw; a shoe-maker's knife or awl
subs: To be tortured, tormented, put to excruciating pain, &c.

def: part. adj. Adorning, gracing (used in comp., e.g. jahān-ārā, 'world-adorning').
part. adj. (f. -ī), Opposed, checked; troublesome, difficult; entangled, &c.
subs: To manage or accomplish a troublesome work; to remove difficulties; to extricate, disentangle; to pay the way
aṛe-kare, aṛe-waqt, s.m. Difficulty; entanglement, complication; adversity, distress.

def: s.m. A saw = آرا ārā, q.v.
s.m. The wandering Arabs of the desert.
s.m. The Arabs or Arabians; an Arabian; — Arabia
subs: s.m. The land of the Arabs, Arabia; — a province of Persia so called.

def: s.m. A wheeled carriage; a gun-carriage; a wagon, a cart.
s.m. A cart, a car, &c.; — (local) a magazine, a depot of arms and ammunition.
s.m. The driver of an ʻarāba, q.v., a carter, &c.
s.m. An Arab of the desert.
(rel. n. fr. ʻarab), m. = P عربيه ʻarabīya (for A. ʻarabīyat), f. adj. & s. Arabian; Arabic; — an Arab; an Arabian horse; — the Arabic language.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عَرَبِی (arabī), from Arabic عَرَبِيّ (ʕarabiyy).

def: (for A. ʻarabīyat), f. = A عربي ʻarabī (rel. n. fr. ʻarab), m. adj. & s. Arabian; Arabic; — an Arab; an Arabian horse; — the Arabic language.
adj. & s.m. Worshipping, adoring, supplicating; conciliating, gratifying; — worshipper, &c.

v.t. To worship, adore, serve; to propitiate; to supplicate; to accomplish.
part. adj. To be worshipped, to be propitiated; venerable, worshipful; — an epithet of a certain class of Brāhmans.
= P. آرا ārā, q.v.
s.m. Purgatory (according to Moḥammadans), a wall intervening between heaven and hell.

ety: for A. عرفة, v.n. fr. عرف 'to know,' &c.
def: s.m. A vigil, wake (esp. that of the night preceding the ʻīduʼẓ-ẓuhạ̄, the ʻīduʼl-fit̤r, and the shab-barāt)
subs: v.n. To keep a vigil, to offer oblations of sweetmeats, &c. on a day of vigil (at the tomb of a deceased relative).

def: s.f. Name of the place or mountain (nine or twelve miles from Mecca) where the pilgrims halt on the day of ʻarafat, i.e. the ninth day of the month ẕuʼl-ḥijja (see ʻarafa).
s.m. Rubbish, sweepings, refuse.
s.f. = اروي arwī, q.v.
s.f. A goad, &c. = آر ar, q.v.; — a churn. s.m. Spoke of a wheel ( = ārā, q.v.). s.m. Spoke of a wheel; a saw; a shoe-maker's knife or awl
subs: To be tortured, tormented, put to excruciating pain, &c.

def: s.f. A goad, &c. = ارئي araʼī, q.v.
s.f. A kind of grass used as fodder for cattle and for thatching.
= اڙهائي aṛhāʼī q.v. adj. Two and a half; two and a half times (e.g. aṛhāʼī sau, two and a half times 100, or 100X2½); — s.f. A full galop.
s.f. Perverseness; crookedness; obstacle; objection; dislike, antipathy, repugnance, prejudice; hostility, enmity
subs: (-se), To have dislike (for), to be prejudiced (against); to bear enmity (to), have a grudge (against)
(-meṅ), To raise objection (to), throw obstacles (in the way); to find fault (with).

def: s.f. Preparation: dressing; ornament, decoration, embellishment; ornamental trees, flowers, &c. of paper or tale and tinsel carried in wedding processions
subs: (-ko), To ornament, embellish, deck, adorn, grace, beautify

def: s.f. pl. (of ʻarīẓa, q.v.), Letters, petitions; representations.
adj. Lame by nature, lame; — s.m. A cripple from birth.
adj. Aryan; of good family, respectable, honourable, venerable.
adj. Without king or ruler; anarchical (a country).
s.f. Coming and going; ingress and egress.
s.m. Sweat; exuded moisture, exudation; moisture, sap, juice, liquor; extract, essence, spirit; the root (of anything)

s.f. Sweating; toil and sweat, hard labour or exertion, severe application
v.n. To be drenched in perspiration
(-kā), To extract the juice, or sap, or liquor (of); to distil
s.m. lit. 'Sweat-receiver'; a skull-cap; — a pad (placed under a saddle)
s.m. Vinegar (so called because mint (naʻnā) is thrown into the sharpest country vinegar to distil it).

def: part. Unprotected, uncared for, undefended.
adj. Reddish; red; blood-red.
s.m. Place of pleasure, garden, grove.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آرَام (ārām), ultimately from Proto-Indo-European *h₁rem-.

ety: rt. Zend ram = S. रम्, with ā
def: s.m. Rest, repose, quiet, ease, relief, comfort, convenience; well-being; health; easy condition or circumstances, competency

v.n. To desire repose, need rest
adj. Quiet or easy in mind
(-ko), To give ease, afford relief; to ease, appease, assuage, relieve; to comfort, soothe, solace; to restore to health, cure, heal
adv. With ease, easily; at (one's) ease, at leisure; comfortably, conveniently, without pain or anxiety
adj. & s.m. Seeking (one's) ease, fond of ease; idle, indolent, lazy
a lover of ease; an indolent or lazy person
ārām lenā, v.n. To take rest; to recover from fatigue, &c.; to rest, repose, recline, lie down, sleep; to loll; to be idle; to live at ease or in enjoyment
v.t. To restore to health, cure, heal
s.f. 1˚ = ārām-wālā; 2˚ A source of ease and comfort, a wife
To derive ease, comfort, convenience, &c. (from, -se); to become well; to become better; to recover; (Met.) to die.

def: s.m. Beginning, commencement; undertaking; effort, exertion
adj. & s.m. = ārām-t̤alab, q.v.s.v. ārām.
part. adj. Reposed; at rest, at ease, quiet.

ety: perf. part. of ārāmīdan; see ārām
def: adj. Quiet, at rest, at ease. (Also āramīda).
(pl. of āramīda), s.m. pl. Those who are at rest, the dead.
s.f. pl. (of ارملة), Widows, distreased and needy women.
s.m. Support, prop, pillar, buttress ( = aṛ, q.v.); — a mode in music; a certain rāginī ( = aḍhānā, or aṛhānā, q.v.).
ex: اس کا پورا سافٹویر ہی اُڑا دیا گیا ہے۔
is kā pūrā sāfṭver hī uṛā diya gayā hai.
The OS has been completely wiped from this (machine).

def: v.t. To cause to stop; to bar progress, bring to a stand-still; to obstruct, impede, check; to ram, thrust, shove, push (against, -se, or into, -meṅ); to stop up, plug, cork; to jam or fasten (one thing to another); to use pressure or force, to force, compel; to suspend, put off, postpone
subs: ( = aṛānā, but generally intensive, e.g.), to ram down tight, to ram home, jam close, block up, &c.; to put off, give up, abandon
ex: اس کا پورا سافٹویر ہی اُڑا دیا گیا ہے۔
is kā pūrā sāfṭver hī uṛā diya gayā hai.
The OS has been completely wiped from this (machine).

def: s.m. The tree from the fruit of which castor-oil is made, Ricinus communis or Palma Christi
subs: adj. 'As or like the root of the Aranḍʼ; unstable, infirm, frail, weak

r: āranda, ārinda, ār, āvar, āvarḍa;, anda, añṭ, anta
ety: ār = āvar; see āvarḍa;+anda = Zend añṭ = S. anta
def: s.m. Porter, carrier.
s.f. The fruit of the castor-oil tree; — a kind of firework, the shell of the castor-oil seed charged with powder
s.m. A market, mart ( = manḍī); a factory, place where goods of any kind are manufactured, a manufacturing town; a warehouse where goods are sold wholesale.
s.m. Obstruction; obstacle, impediment; prop, support, buttress; — entangling the legs so as to trip (in wrestling); tripping or giving a cross-buttock (in wrestling)
subs: (-ko), To have an adversary in such a position (in wrestling) that it is easy to throw him; to have on the hip, have an advantage over; to bring under one's thumb; to trick, circumvent
or de-mārnā, To throw one by tripping, or across the hip
(-meṅ), To place an obstacle (in the way of), to hinder, impede; to place a prop (under), to prop up, support
aṛangā lagānā, v.t. To trip up.

def: s.f. Nonsensical talk, gibberish; folly ( = aṛ-baṛ)
subs: v.n. To talk nonsense, to prate; to rave.

def: s.f. A parasol, umbrella; a large fan (local aḍyānī).
s.f. Wood used for kindling fire by attrition (commonly that of the Ficus religiosa).
s.m. Forest; wilderness, desert.
s.m. = ارنيه araṇya; adj. Forest, relating to a forest, forest-born, wild
subs: s.f. Forest-court, a court for a community of hermits
s.f. A certain religious ceremony observed by women
s.m. The title of the third book of the Rāmāyaṇ.

def: s.f. Scramble, scrimmage, scuffle, tussle; scramble for means of living; need, necessity
subs: To be in a state of penury and want, to be hard up.

def: s.m. Shelter; a shed or hut in the jungles used by graziers.
s.m. Heap, pile, place where wood, &c. is heaped or collected ( = aṭālā); harvest floor for the blossoms of the mahnʼā; outhouses for cattle ( = arār); high, steep bank ( = aṛāṛā).
s.m.(?), The juniper; — the cypress (syn. saro).
s.m. Bump or swelling occasioned by the sting or bite of an insect; nail-mark left after scratching; — steep, high bank of a river ( = kaṛāṛā, which is the more com. form).
s.m. High. steep bank of a river, &c.; (the more com. form is kaṛāṛa).
v.n. To scream out. (Cf. arrānā).
v.n. = اڙانا aṛṛānā, q.v. v.n. To produce a prolonged rumbling sound (as a mill, fall of a building, &c.).
s.f. The measure of a field originally established in a lease or grant to which the occupant appeals in preference to an actual measurement or adjustment.
s.m. Arrowroot.
adj. Without juice, sapless; tasteless, insipid; useless; vapid, dull, flat (as a composition).
= آلس ālas, q.v. s.m. Inactivity, lack of energy, indolence, laziness, idleness, sloth; drowsiness; — adj. Lazy, slothful, indolent; drowsy.

ety: abst. subst. from āṛā; cf. mīṭhās, from mīṭhā
def: s.f. Scantiness (of room), confinedness, narrowness, closeness.
adj. = آلسی ālasī, q.v. adj. Inactive, lazy, indolent, slothful; drowsy; weak; — s.m. A lazy person, sluggard, &c.

s.m. = ارش پرش arś-parś, q.v. s.m. Touching; touch, contact; partial bathing, throwing a little water on the head with one's hand; sprinkling, aspersion; defilement or impurity arising from contact (with anything unclean)
subs: (or aras-paras) honā or ho-jānā (-se), To be touched (by) and thus rendered impure, to be defiled or polluted (by).

ety: ā+Pehl. rāst; rt. Z. rād = S. राध्
def: adj. Prepared; arranged, disposed; well-ordered, well-regulated; regular; improved; adorned, embellished, decorated, bedecked (with); dressed; equipped
subs: adj. Bedecked, adorned with jewels, &c.; equipped

v.t. To prepare, get ready; to put in order, arrange, dispose, adjust, regulate, settle; to draw up in order (an army), to array; to collect, group; to adorn, ornament, decorate, embellish, beautify, grace.

def: s.f. Preparation; putting in order, arrangement, disposition, adjustment; regularity, order; decoration, ornament, embellishment.
s.m. Insipidity, want of flavour; spiritlessness.

= آڙهت āṛhat, q.v. s.m. f. (?), A well-wheel, the (so-called) Persian wheel (a string of earthen pots attached to a revolving wheel over a well, which go down empty and come up full and tilt the water into a trough: — The commoner form of the word is رهٿ rahaṭ).
corr. for ارتهہ arth, q.v.
s.m. Enemy, foe.
s.m. Sound, noise.

ety: redupl. of aṛāẉā, v.n. of aṛāwanā = aṛānā
def: s.m. Taunts, jeers; inuendoes; insults
s.m. The lotus, Nymphæa nelumbo.
s.f. A thing that happens to or befalls (one), an accident, disease, sickness, a bane, a cause of mischief; — a contingent or accidental quality; — a muster of troops ( = ʻarẓ).
adv. Accidentally, by chance.
s.f. pl. (of ارض arẓ), Lands, estates; detached portions of land.
incor. for اراذل arāẕil, q.v.
s.m. pl. (of ارذل arẕul, the pl. of رذل raẕl), The base, the low or vulgar; mean people.
s.f. pl. of, and = اراضي arāẓī, q.v. s.f. pl. (of ارض arẓ), Lands, estates; detached portions of land.
adj. One hundred millions
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit अर्बुद (arbuda). Compare Nepali अरब (arab), Punjabi ਅਰਬ (arab)/ارب, Sindhi ارب (ārb).

def: adj. Four.
subs: or arbaʻ mutanāsiba, Simple Rule of Three
s.m. The four elements, earth, air, fire, water.

def: adj. Four.
subs: or arbaʻ mutanāsiba, Simple Rule of Three
s.m. The four elements, earth, air, fire, water.

r: arbāb, rabb, arbāb-i-baṣīrat, arbāb-i-jāh, arbāb-i-dānish, arbāb-i-sukhan, arbāb-i-sharʻ, arbāb-i-firāsat, arbāb-i-fahm, arbāb-i-māl, arbāb-i-maʻnī, arbāb-i-nishāt̤, arbāb-i-himmat
def: s.m. pl. (of رب rabb), Possessors, lords, masters
subs: The intelligent, the clear-sighted; the circumspect
Those who are possessed of dignity
The eloquent; masters of language, the poets
The acute; the penetrating; the sagacious
Men of spirit, men of lofty aspirations.

ety: for A. عربدة, inf. n. of عربد 'to show ill-nature,' &c.
def: s.m. Ill-nature, evil disposition; antipathy; dispute, conflict.
adj. Forty.
adj. Low, inferior, vile
s.f. Age, life, duration of life.
v.s.v. آڙ āṛ.
adj. Crooked, uneven; — s.m. Obstruction; bar, obstacle, impediment
subs: (-meṅ), To place an obstacle, &c. (in the way of), to obstruct, impede.

def: = H اڙبڙنگ अड़बड़ंग aṛ-baṛ-ang, v.s.v. اڙ aṛ.

v.n. = هڙبڙانا haṛbaṛānā, q.v. v.n. To be flurried, to be confused, to be agitated; to be in consternation; — to be in a hurry; to hurry; to be precipitate; — to start up from a troubled dream or sleep; to be fidgety, or restless; — to rumble (as the bowels); — v.t. To flurry, to confuse, to perplex.
= H اڙبر अड़बड़ aṛ-baṛ, v.s.v. اڙ aṛ.
adj. Forty.
s.m. Boy, child, son; the young of any animal.
s.m. A lotus, Nelumbium speciosum, or Nymphæa nelumbo.
adj. One hundred millions (see arab); the shape of the fœtus in the second month after conception; a certain mountain.
s.f. Adoration, worship; devotion; an image.
s.f. Adoration, worship; devotion; an image.
adj. & s.m. Worshipping, adoring; — worshipper.
s.m. = S ارچنا अर्चना arćanā, s.f., Worship, adoration; homage, reverence, respect; ceremoniousness.
s.f., = S ارچن अर्चन arćan, s.m. Worship, adoration; homage, reverence, respect; ceremoniousness.
s.m. Flame; light, radiance, splendour.
part. Worshipped, adored, revered.
v.t. To worship, adore.
s.m. Flour; meal.

ety: by met. for adlā, from badlā
def: s.m. (prov.), Requital, return, retaliation.
s. Lit. 'like Paradise'; the second month of the Persian calendar, corresponding to April.
s.m. An orderly; a peon in regular attendance on an official; an attendant who runs before his master's conveyance.
s.f. Posse or crowd of suitors or dissolute fellows
subs: (-par), A crowd of lewd fellows to fall (on, — commonly applied to a rush made upon a harlot by a number of loose men).

def: s.m. A begging request; covetousness; — wounding, killing.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اُرْدُن (urdun), itself from Arabic الْأُرْدُنّ (al-ʔurdunn).

ety: S. अर्दना+आशम्; but cf. with عرض داشت, of which it is prob. a corr.
def: s.f. Representation, petition, request, entreaty; supplication; offering (to a deity)
subs: v.t. To petition, solicit, request, entreat, beseech; to worship; to pray.

def: s.m. A mixture of parched and bruised grain and barley (or wheat and barley) given as food to cattle, either in a dry state or mixed with water.
= ارداوا ardāwā, q.v. s.m. A mixture of parched and bruised grain and barley (or wheat and barley) given as food to cattle, either in a dry state or mixed with water.
adj. & s.m. Half-, semi-, hemi-; a half, a part
subs: ardhang (i.e. ardha+anga), s.m. Lit. 'Half the body'; palsy affecting half the body, hemiplegia; distortion of the face
ardhangī, adj. & s.m. Afflicted with hemiplegia; — one so afflicted, a paralytic; an appellation of the god Śiva
s.f. A wife; — a slovenly woman
(arddha-) ćandra, s.m. Half-moon; crescent; semicircle (v.s.v. adh)
s.m. Hemistich; a line in epic poetry
s.m. Lit. 'Half-kumbha,' the sixth year fair (see kumbhanī).

def: = اردن ardan, q.v. s.m. A begging request; covetousness; — wounding, killing.
adj. Wet, moist, damp.
s.f. Name of one of the lunar mansions ( = ādrā, q.v.). s.m. The sixth Nakshatra or lunar mansion; the beginning of the rainy season.
s.m. Green ginger ( = adrak, q.v.). s.f. Green ginger (Amomum zinziber).
intj. (used chiefly in calling to or addressing inferiors), Holla! ho! O! Sirrah! hark ye!
subs: intj. Good gracious! O dear, O dear! What are you about!
intj. Alas! O dear! Woe's me!

def: adv. Yes, yea, verily
subs: s.m. Evasion, shift, shuffling, subterfuge, deception.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀆𑀭𑀸 (ārā) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀇𑀓𑀸- (-ikā-), from Sanskrit आरा॑ (ā́rā).

def: = H آڙي آنا आड़ेआना āṛe-ānā, see aṛe, s.v. اڙا aṛā.
= H اڙي آنا अड़ेआना aṛe-ānā, see aṛe, s.v. اڙا aṛā.
v.s.v. P. آرے are.
adv. On this side and that; right and left. (See ār-pār.)
s.f. Wealth; property; effects.
adj. compar. & super. (of رفيع rafīʻ), Higher; highest; very high; most exalted; sublime.
s.m. An obstinate heretic or schismatic, particularly a bigoted Shīʻa.
adj. = الگا algā, q.v. adj. Separate, apart, distinct; free, loose, unconfined, &c. (see alag): — s.m. A sandal.
s.f. = الگائي algāʼī, q.v. s.f. Separation; holding or keeping aloof, non-interference; being silent.
s.m. A perfume of a yellowish colour compounded of several scented ingredients: (One receipt specifies sandal, rose-water, camphor, musk, ambergris and butter as the ingredients).
adj. Dyed with argajā (garments, &c.).
s.m. A wooden bolt or pin for fastening a door; a bar.
s.m. An organ.
v.t. = الگانا algānā, q.v. v.t. = alag karnā, q.v.s.v. alag; to put on one side; to put up, raise.
s.f. = الگني alganī, q.v.
s.m. An organ,
s.m. A plant whose flowers and fruit are of a beautiful red (Arbor Judæa); red colour, crimson; purple.
adj. Red, crimson; purple.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ارغوانی (arġawānī). By surface analysis, ارغوان (arġavān) + ـی (-ī).

def: s.m. A mode of worship; libation; a respectful offering of various ingredients to a god or a Brāhman; — price, cost
subs: (or aragh) ćaṛhānā, ćaṛhā-denā, (-ko), To make an oblation to a deity, &c.

def: s.m. A small boat-shaped vessel used by the Hindūs in their devotions, for performing libations.
s.m. Rubbish, &c. ( = āṛā-goṛā); transactions, dealings; account.
s.f. A kind of fish. (Cf. aṛwāṛī).
adj. Two and a half; two and a half times (e.g. aṛhāʼī sau, two and a half times 100, or 100X2½); — s.f. A full galop.
s.f. A weight or measure of two and a half seers; the multiplication table or pahārā of 2½ times.
adj. compar. & super. (of رحيم raḥīm), More, most, or very merciful or compassionate
subs: The most merciful of the merciful (an attribute of God).
